title: willoughbya hirsutissima
intro: Willoughbya hirsutissima, commonly known as the hairy willow-herb, is a plant species in the family Plantaginaceae. It is a perennial herb with hairy leaves and stems, native to Australia and New Zealand. The health benefits of Willoughbya hirsutissima include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which exhibit therapeutic actions such as antioxidant and anti-cancer activities.
